relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Justice loses its purity when performed for applause.
Mercy-giving is not theater for human honor.
True generosity becomes so free it does not even watch itself.
The Father sees hidden mercy.
Prayer is not performance.
The secret room becomes a meeting place with the Father.
Many words are not the same as communion.
Need is already known before speech.
Prayer begins by hallowing the Father in the heavens.
Heaven’s reign is asked to become earth’s reality.
Bread is asked for today, enough for the path.
Release is received and extended as debt is forgiven.
The soul asks not to be led into testing but rescued from harm.
Release opens release.
Refusal to release blocks the flow of release.
Fasting too can become performance if the face is staged.
Hidden fasting carries quiet dignity.
The Father sees what is done in secret.
Earthly treasure can decay or be taken.
Heavenly treasure is not consumed or stolen.
The heart follows treasure.
A clear and generous eye fills the body with light.
A distorted eye turns inner light into darkness.
Two masters divide the soul; the Divine and Mammon cannot both rule.
Life is more than food, and the body more than clothing.
Birds live inside the Father’s provision without barns.
Worry cannot add life.
Lilies teach growth without anxious striving.
Their simple clothing outshines Solomon’s glory.
If field grass is clothed, trust can soften.
Food, drink, and clothing are real needs, but not the soul’s master.
The Father knows the needs before they are named.
Seek first divine reign and justice; provision comes into order.
Tomorrow need not be carried before it arrives.
